7.1:
This is what the Lord Yahweh showed me:
he was forming swarms of locusts
at the moment the late grass began to shoot up —
the second crop that comes after the king takes the first cutting.
7.2:
When the locusts had stripped every blade of grass from the land,
I cried out: 'O Lord Yahweh, forgive!
How will Jacob survive? He is so small!'
7.3:
Yahweh relented.
'This will not happen,' said Yahweh.
7.4:
This is what the Lord Yahweh showed me:
the Lord Yahweh was calling for judgment by fire —
it swallowed up the great deep
and was beginning to devour the land itself.
7.5:
I cried out again: 'Stop, O Lord Yahweh!
How will Jacob survive? He is so small!'
7.6:
Yahweh relented of this too.
'This will not happen either,' said the Lord Yahweh.
7.7:
This is what he showed me:
the Lord was standing beside a wall built straight by a plumb line,
and he was holding a plumb line in his hand.
7.8:
'Amos, what do you see?' Yahweh asked.
'A plumb line,' I answered.
Then the Lord declared:
'I am setting a plumb line against my people Israel —
they do not measure up.
I will not spare them again.'
7.10:
Then Amaziah, the priest of Bethel, sent a message to Jeroboam king of Israel:
'Amos is raising a conspiracy against you right here in Israel.
The land cannot bear everything he is saying.'
7.11:
For Amos has been saying:
'Jeroboam will die by the sword,
and Israel will be carried into exile from its own land.'
7.12:
Amaziah then spoke directly to Amos:
'Get out of here, prophet — go back to Judah.
Earn your living there and do your prophesying there.'
7.13:
But you will not prophesy here at Bethel again —
this is the king's own sanctuary, a royal house.
7.14:
Amos answered Amaziah:
'I am not a prophet. I belong to no prophet's guild.
I am a sheep-breeder — a dresser of sycamore figs.
I have no professional standing here.'
7.15:
But Yahweh took me from behind the flock
and Yahweh said to me:
'Go — prophesy to my people Israel.'
7.17:
Therefore this is what Yahweh says:
Your wife will become a prostitute right in this city.
Your sons and daughters will be cut down by the sword.
Your land will be marked off and handed out to strangers.
You yourself will die in an unclean, foreign land —
and Israel will be dragged into exile from its own homeland.

8.1:
This is what the Lord Yahweh showed me:
a basket of ripe summer fruit.
8.2:
'Amos, what do you see?' he asked.
'A basket of ripe summer fruit,' I said.
Then Yahweh said:
'Summer fruit is ripe — and so is the end.
The end has come upon my people Israel;
I will not spare them again.'
8.3:
'On that day,' declares the Lord Yahweh,
'the singing in the temple will turn to wailing.
Corpses will be everywhere —
cast out in every direction.
Nothing but silence.'
8.4:
Listen to this — you who crush the needy
and grind down the poor of the land —
8.5:
You cannot wait for the holy days to end.
'When will the new moon be over?
When will the sabbath pass
so we can put our wheat on the market —
with rigged measures and inflated prices
and scales tipped against the buyer?'
8.6:
Buying the desperate poor for a handful of coins,
the needy for a pair of sandals —
and then selling them the swept-up floor-dust as if it were grain.
8.7:
Yahweh has taken an oath by the pride of Jacob himself:
'Not one of these deeds will I forget — not one.'
8.8:
Will the land not shake for this?
Will not everyone in it grieve?
The whole earth will heave like the Nile in flood —
swelling, churning, then sinking,
the way the Nile rises and falls.
8.9:
'On that day,' declares the Lord Yahweh,
'I will bring the sun down at noon —
I will drench the earth in darkness at midday.'
8.10:
'I will turn your festivals into funerals
and every song into a dirge.
Sackcloth on every body; shaved heads everywhere.
The grief will be like a father's grief for his only son —
and it will end as bitterly as it began.'
8.11:
'The days are coming,' declares the Lord Yahweh,
'when I will send a famine across the land —
not hunger for bread, not thirst for water,
but a famine of hearing Yahweh's own word.'
8.12:
People will stagger from sea to sea,
from north to east, searching everywhere —
running to and fro, desperately seeking a word from Yahweh.
They will not find one.
8.13:
The finest young women, the strongest young men —
on that day they will collapse from thirst.
8.14:
All who swear by the idol-shame of Samaria,
who take their oaths by the calf at Dan —
'As your god lives!' —
or make their vows by the pilgrimage at Beersheba —
they will fall and never get up again.

9.1:
I saw the Lord himself standing beside the altar.
He commanded:
'Strike the capitals — let the thresholds shudder!
Bring them down on the heads of all who are there.
Whoever survives I will hunt down with the sword.
Not one shall make it out. Not one.'
9.2:
'They can dig all the way down to the world of the dead —
my hand will reach them there.
They can climb to the highest heaven —
I will drag them back down.'
9.3:
'They can conceal themselves on the summit of Carmel —
I will hunt them down and drag them out.
If they sink to the bottom of the sea to hide from me,
I will send the sea-serpent after them.
It will bite them.'
9.4:
'Even if their enemies march them into exile,
I will send the sword after them there —
it will cut them down.
I will keep my eye on them —
but for disaster, not for good.'
9.5:
The Lord Yahweh of armies —
he touches the earth and it dissolves;
every one living on it mourns.
The whole land heaves like the Nile,
rises and falls like Egypt's great river —
9.6:
the one who builds his high palace in the heavens
and sets its foundations upon the earth,
who summons the waters of the sea
and pours them across the face of the earth —
Yahweh is his name.
9.7:
'Do you think you are uniquely precious to me, O Israel?' declares Yahweh.
'Yes, I brought you up from Egypt —
but I also brought the Philistines from Caphtor
and the Syrians from Kir.
You are no more to me than the Cushites.'
9.8:
'Look — the eyes of the Lord Yahweh are fixed
on this sinful kingdom.
I will wipe it off the face of the earth.
And yet — I will not completely annihilate the house of Jacob,'
declares Yahweh.
9.9:
'Here is what I will do:
I will shake the house of Israel through every nation —
the way grain is shaken in a sieve.
But not one good kernel will be lost to the ground.'
9.10:
'Every sinner among my people will die by the sword —
especially those who keep telling themselves:
"Nothing bad will ever reach us. Disaster cannot touch us."'
9.11:
'On that day I will raise up
the fallen shelter of David —
patch every broken wall,
lift up what has collapsed,
and rebuild it as it stood in days of old —'
9.12:
'so that Israel may reclaim the remnant of Edom
and all the nations over whom my name has been called,'
declares Yahweh, who is doing all of this.
9.13:
'The days are coming,' declares Yahweh,
'when the plowman will catch up with the reaper —
so fast will the harvests follow —
and the grape-treader will overtake the one who sows the seed.
The mountains will run with new wine;
every hill will flow with it.'
9.14:
'I will bring my people Israel back from captivity.
They will rebuild the cities left in ruins and settle in them.
They will plant vineyards — and drink the wine.
They will cultivate gardens — and eat what they grow.'
9.15:
'I will plant them firmly in their own land,
and they will never be pulled up from it again —
from the land I have given them,'
says Yahweh your God.

1.1: These are the words of Amos — a sheep breeder from Tekoa — the vision he received concerning Israel, in the reign of Uzziah over Judah and Jeroboam son of Joash over Israel, two years before the earthquake struck.
1.2: He declared: Yahweh roars from Zion, / his voice thunders out of Jerusalem — / the shepherds' meadows turn to mourning, / the crown of Carmel shrivels and dies.
1.3: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Damascus will not escape judgment, for they ground Gilead to dust beneath iron threshing sledges.
1.4: I will send fire on Hazael's dynasty, and it will devour the citadels of Ben-hadad.
1.5: The gate-bar of Damascus I will shatter; / the lord of the Valley of Aven will be cut down, / the scepter-bearer of Beth-eden driven out — / and Aram's people will be exiled to Kir. / Yahweh has spoken.
1.6: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Gaza will not escape judgment, for they uprooted whole communities and handed them over to Edom.
1.7: I will send fire against Gaza's walls — it will devour her citadels to ash.
1.8: The lord of Ashdod I will cut down; / the scepter of Ashkelon will be broken; / I will turn against Ekron — / and the last of the Philistines will be destroyed. / So says the Sovereign LORD.
1.9: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Tyre will not escape judgment, for they sold whole peoples into Edom's hands and trampled the covenant that bound them as brothers.
1.10: I will send fire against the walls of Tyre — it will consume her citadels.
1.11: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Edom will not escape judgment, for he hunted down his own brother with the sword, killed all compassion within himself, and kept his rage burning — an undying fury nursed through every generation.
1.12: I will send fire on Teman — it will devour the citadels of Bozrah.
1.13: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Ammon will not escape judgment, for they ripped open pregnant women in Gilead — a war crime against the unborn — all to push their border wider.
1.14: I will kindle fire against the walls of Rabbah — her citadels will burn amid the war-shouts of battle, in the roar of the whirlwind.
1.15: Their king will be marched into exile — he and all his princes together. Yahweh has spoken.

2.1: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Moab will not escape judgment, for they burned the bones of Edom's king to lime — desecrating the dead.
2.2: I will send fire on Moab — the citadels of Kerioth will burn; / Moab will die in the din of battle, / in shouts and the blast of the trumpet.
2.3: I will cut down the ruler from among them, and every prince will fall with him. Yahweh has spoken.
2.4: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Judah will not escape judgment, for they have spurned the LORD's Torah and refused his statutes; they have been led astray by the same false gods their fathers chased.
2.5: I will send fire on Judah — it will devour Jerusalem's citadels.
2.6: Thus says Yahweh: Three crimes, four — and Israel will not escape judgment, for they sell the righteous man for silver and the destitute for a pair of sandals.
2.7: They grind the poor man's face into the dust; / they shove the afflicted off the road. / Father and son share the same girl — / defiling my holy name.
2.8: On garments seized as collateral they sprawl beside every altar; / in the temple of their own God they drink wine wrung from the poor through court fines.
2.9: And yet — I was the one who destroyed the Amorite before them, / tall as cedars, strong as oaks; / I cut off his fruit above / and his roots below.
2.10: I brought you up from Egypt; / I led you through the wilderness forty years / to put you in possession of the Amorite's land.
2.11: I raised up prophets from among your own sons, / Nazirites from your own young men. / Is this not true, Israel? / So Yahweh declares.
2.12: But you gave the Nazirites wine to drink / and silenced the prophets: 'Do not prophesy.'
2.13: Now watch — I will press you down where you stand, / as a loaded harvest cart crushes the grain beneath it.
2.14: The fastest runner will have nowhere to flee; / the strongest man will find his strength gone; / the warrior will not save his own life.
2.15: The archer will not stand his ground; / the fast-footed soldier will not escape; / the mounted warrior will not save his life.
2.16: Even the bravest of soldiers / will run away naked on that day. / Yahweh has spoken.

3.1: Hear this word that Yahweh has spoken against you, O people of Israel — against the whole family he brought up from Egypt:
3.2: You — only you — of all the families of the earth have I known in covenant. / That is exactly why I must call every sin of yours to account.
3.3: Do two people travel together unless they have arranged to meet?
3.4: Does a lion roar in the forest when there is no prey? / Does the lion's cub snarl from its den if it has caught nothing?
3.5: Does a bird fall to the ground in a trap / unless a snare was set for it? / Does a trap spring shut / unless it has caught something?
3.6: When the trumpet sounds the alarm in a city, don't the people tremble? / When disaster strikes a city — hasn't the LORD done it?
3.7: The Sovereign LORD does nothing in the world without first revealing his purpose to his servants the prophets.
3.8: A lion has roared — who would not be afraid? / The Sovereign LORD has spoken — who can help but prophesy?
3.9: Announce it to the citadels of Ashdod, / proclaim it to the strongholds of Egypt: / 'Come, gather on the hills of Samaria — / see the chaos that seethes within her, / see the crushed people in her midst.'
3.10: They have lost all knowledge of what is right — so Yahweh declares — / they who stuff their citadels with violence and robbery.
3.11: And so — this is the word of the Sovereign LORD: / An enemy will encircle the land; / your military power will be stripped away, / your citadels looted.
3.12: Thus says Yahweh: The way a shepherd grabs from a lion's jaws / two bone-legs and a torn ear-scrap, / so will it be for the Israelites who live in Samaria — / rescued with nothing but a corner of a couch, / a splinter of a bed.
3.13: Hear and bear witness against the house of Jacob — so says the Sovereign LORD, the God of heavenly armies.
3.14: On the day I bring Israel to judgment for its sins, / I will strike at the altars of Bethel — / the altar horns will be hacked off / and topple to the ground.
3.15: I will smash the winter palace together with the summer retreat; / the ivory-paneled mansions will be brought down; / the great houses — all of them — will end. / Yahweh has spoken.

4.1: Hear this, you pampered cows of Bashan / lounging on the hills of Samaria — / you who grind the poor underfoot / and demand of your husbands: 'Bring us drinks!'
4.2: The Sovereign LORD swears it by his own holiness: / Days are coming upon you / when they will drag you out with hooks — / drag every last one of you away with fishhooks.
4.3: Through the gaping breaches in the walls you will be shoved, / one by one, straight out before you, / and hurled toward Harmon. / Yahweh has spoken.
4.4: Come on then — go to Bethel and sin, / go to Gilgal and sin all the more! / Bring your sacrifices every morning, / your tithes every three days.
4.5: Burn leavened bread as a thanksgiving offering; / announce your freewill gifts, trumpet them about — / because this is what you love to do, Israel! / So says the Sovereign LORD.
4.6: I struck you with famine — / empty mouths in every city, / no bread anywhere in the land — / and still you did not come back to me. / Yahweh's word.
4.7: I held back the rain from you / when the harvest was three months away — / sending rain on one city / but withholding it from another; / one field drenched, another parched.
4.8: Two cities, three, staggered to another city just to find water, / and still went thirsty — / yet you did not come back to me. / Yahweh's word.
4.9: I struck your crops with blight and mildew; / locust after locust stripped your gardens, / your vineyards, your fig trees, your olives — / and still you did not come back to me. / Yahweh's word.
4.10: I sent plague through your ranks like Egypt's plagues; / I cut down your young men with the sword; / I led your horses away as plunder / and made the stench of your camps rise to your nostrils — / and still you did not come back to me. / Yahweh's word.
4.11: I overthrew some of you the way God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah; / you were like a smoldering stick yanked from the fire — / and still you did not come back to me. / Yahweh's word.
4.12: Therefore this is what I will do to you, Israel — / and because I am doing this to you, / prepare yourself to face your God, O Israel.
4.13: For he is the one who shapes the mountains / and stirs the wind into being, / who tells a human being what he is pondering — / who turns the dawn to darkness / and strides over the high places of the earth: / Yahweh, the God of heavenly armies — that is his name.

5.1: Hear this word — this funeral dirge I raise over you, house of Israel:
5.2: She has fallen and will not rise again — / the virgin Israel; / she lies abandoned on her land, / and no one comes to help her up.
5.3: For this is what the Sovereign LORD says: / The city that once sent out a thousand soldiers — / a hundred will come home. / The city that sent a hundred — / ten will survive for the house of Israel.
5.4: This is what Yahweh says to the house of Israel: Seek me, and you will live.
5.5: Do not go to Bethel; / do not go to Gilgal; / do not make the journey to Beersheba — / for Gilgal is going into exile for certain, / and Bethel — the house of God — will become nothing at all.
5.6: Seek Yahweh and live — / or he will sweep like fire through the house of Joseph, / devouring it with no one there to quench it — / no one to save Bethel.
5.7: You who warp justice into bitter wormwood / and throw righteousness into the dirt —
5.8: — seek him who made the Pleiades and Orion, / who turns midnight into dawn / and darkens the day to night, / who calls for the waters of the sea / and pours them across the face of the earth — / Yahweh is his name.
5.9: He sends ruin flashing against the powerful, / so that destruction falls on the fortress.
5.10: They hate the one who challenges injustice at the city gate; / they loathe the one who speaks straight.
5.11: Therefore — because you grind the poor into the ground / and levy grain taxes on their backs — / you have built houses of fine-cut stone, / but you will never live in them; / you have planted lush vineyards, / but you will never drink their wine.
5.12: I know exactly how many crimes you have — how great your sins are — / you who crush the innocent, take bribes, / and push the poor aside at the gate.
5.13: And so the wise stay silent in such a time — / because it is a time of evil.
5.14: Seek good and not evil — then you will live; / and the LORD, the God of heavenly armies, will be with you, / as you keep saying he already is.
5.15: Hate evil, love good, / make justice stand at the city gate — / perhaps then Yahweh, the God of heavenly armies, / will show grace to what remains of Joseph.
5.16: And so — this is what Yahweh, the God of heavenly armies, my Lord, declares: / Wailing in every public square; / 'Alas! Alas!' in every street. / The farmers will be called to mourn; / those who know lamentation will be called to lead it.
5.17: Wailing even in the vineyards — / for I will pass through your midst. / Yahweh has spoken.
5.18: Woe to those who crave the Day of the LORD! / What do you expect from the Day of the LORD? / It is darkness — not light.
5.19: Like a man who runs from a lion / and is met by a bear — / who escapes into his house / and leans his hand against the wall / only to be bitten by a serpent.
5.20: Is the Day of the LORD not darkness, not light — / absolute darkness, without a ray of brightness?
5.21: I hate, I utterly despise your festivals; / I take no pleasure in your solemn assemblies.
5.22: Even if you bring me burnt offerings and grain offerings, / I will not receive them; / even your choice fellowship offerings — / I will not look at them.
5.23: Stop the racket of your songs — / I will not listen to the music of your harps.
5.24: Instead — let justice roll down like a river, / and righteousness like a stream that never runs dry.
5.25: Was it sacrifices and offerings you brought me / in the forty years of the wilderness, house of Israel?
5.26: No — you carried your idol-shrines: / Sikkuth your king, / Kiyyun your star-god — / images you had made for yourselves.
5.27: Therefore I will drive you into exile beyond Damascus — / so says Yahweh, whose name is the God of heavenly armies.

6.1: Woe to those lounging at ease in Zion, / trusting in the security of Samaria's hill — / the leading men of the first among nations, / to whom Israel itself looks.
6.2: Go look at Calneh; / go to Hamath the great; / go down to Gath of the Philistines. / Are those kingdoms any better than yours? / Is their territory wider than yours?
6.3: You who push the day of disaster out of your minds / while bringing the seat of violence nearer —
6.4: who lounge on ivory-inlaid beds / and sprawl across their couches, / feasting on lambs from the flock / and veal from the fattening stalls —
6.5: who strum away on the harp / and compose songs for themselves like David —
6.6: who drink wine by the bowlful / and douse themselves with expensive oils — / but they feel nothing / for the ruin of Joseph.
6.7: And so they will be the first ones led away into exile — / the banqueting of those who sprawled will be over.
6.8: The Sovereign LORD has sworn — by himself — / so Yahweh, the God of heavenly armies, declares: / I am revolted by Jacob's pride; / I hate his citadels. / I will hand over the city and everything in it.
6.9: And if ten people survive in one house, they will all die.
6.10: When a relative comes to burn the body and carry the bones from the house, / and calls to someone still hiding inside: 'Is there anyone else with you?' — / the answer comes back: 'No, no one.' / Then he says: 'Silence — / we must not invoke the name of Yahweh.'
6.11: For the LORD has issued his command: / the great house will be smashed to rubble; / the small house, to splinters.
6.12: Horses don't gallop on bare rock; / oxen don't plow stone. / But you have turned justice into poison / and righteousness into bitter wormwood.
6.13: You who celebrate your conquest of Lo-debar — Nothing! — / and boast: 'Have we not seized Karnaim, the Two Horns, / by our own power?'
6.14: For look — I am raising up a nation against you, house of Israel — / so Yahweh, the God of heavenly armies, declares — / and they will press you hard / from the entrance of Hamath to the Wadi of the Arabah.
